summary: PR 588 was a build/decompilation hygiene fix aimed at making m2c-derived decompilation work cleaner. It renamed Captain Falcon symbols from the older ftCFalcon prefix to the project-consistent ftCaptain prefix across assembly labels, ftdata callback tables, and C prototypes; moved broad declarations out of src/functions.h into more specific fighter headers; added a private __ftCaptain.h for Captain-internal/Ganon-shared declarations; changed Samus helper functions to file-local static declarations; cleaned a malformed assembly comment; and reworked HUD percent-status structs/names in ifstatus to better describe damage digit state and support a NON_MATCHING C reconstruction of func_802F491C while preserving the asm fallback.
what_changed: Renamed Captain Falcon callback symbols from ftCFalcon_* to ftCaptain_* in assembly labels and C declarations/definitions, including ftCaptain_SpecialLw_StartAction, ftCaptain_SpecialAirLw_StartAction, ftCaptain_SpecialAirHi_StartAction, ftCaptain_OnItemPickup, ftCaptain_OnItemInvisible, ftCaptain_OnItemVisible, ftCaptain_OnItemDrop, and ftCaptain_LoadSpecialAttrs.
Updated ftdata.s callback tables to point at the renamed ftCaptain_* symbols for special moves, item callbacks, and LoadSpecialAttrs.
Removed several broad fighter declarations from src/functions.h and relocated relevant declarations to more specific headers such as ftCaptain/__ftCaptain.h and ft_unknown_006.h.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCaptain/__ftCaptain.h as a private/internal Captain header that includes ftCaptain.h and declares ftCaptain_OnLoadForGanon and ftCaptain_LoadSpecialAttrs.
Updated ftCaptain.c, ftCaptain_SpecialN.c, ftCaptain_SpecialS.c, and ftGanon.c to include the new __ftCaptain.h where private Captain declarations are needed.
Changed ftGanon_LoadSpecialAttrs to call ftCaptain_LoadSpecialAttrs instead of the old ftCFalcon_LoadSpecialAttrs symbol.
Changed ftSamus_80128AC8 and ftSamus_80128B1C from globally declared functions to static file-local helpers in ftsamus2.c, with forward declarations at the top of the file.
Adjusted ftsamus2.c formatting and control-flow style without clear evidence of semantic changes in the excerpt.
Changed code_80081B38.h declaration for func_80084FA8 from asm void to a normal C prototype.
Fixed a corrupted asm comment/address in ft_unknown_006.c where a line contained '00ftPurin_SpecialHi_StartAction0B2280' instead of the normal address/comment format.
Added prototypes for func_800DEEE8, func_800DEF38, func_800DF0D0, and func_800DEEA8 to ft_unknown_006.h.
Renamed ifstatus HUD damage structs from generic HudFlags/HudValue to IfDamageFlags/IfDamageState and renamed unk40 to randomize_velocity.
Introduced IfDamagePlace enum and array-based fields for HUD digit translation, velocity, and JObj pointers in ifstatus.h, replacing individually named hundreds/tens/ones/percent fields.

Added a NON_MATCHING C reconstruction scaffold for func_802F491C in ifstatus.c with local helper types, inline JObj translation helpers, assert macro use matching jobj.h assert strings, and an asm fallback under #else.
smart_moves: Kept the func_802F491C reconstruction behind NON_MATCHING while retaining the existing asm implementation, allowing decompilation experimentation without risking a matching/build regression.
Moved declarations out of the global functions.h catch-all into domain-specific headers, improving include hygiene and reducing accidental global symbol exposure.
Used a private __ftCaptain.h for declarations needed by Captain implementation and Ganon sharing, avoiding pollution of the public ftCaptain.h surface where possible.
Renamed ftCFalcon symbols to ftCaptain consistently across assembly globals, C symbols, and ftdata function-pointer tables, which avoids linker/name mismatches after decompilation.
Marked Samus helper functions static when they are only used inside ftsamus2.c, which helps match intended linkage and prevents unnecessary global prototypes.
Refactored repeated HUD digit fields into indexed arrays with HUD_PLACE_MAX, making the structure layout clearer while preserving offsets shown in comments.
Preserved evidence comments around hardcoded constants and assert line numbers in the NON_MATCHING ifstatus reconstruction, useful for later matching work.
decomp_lessons: When m2c output depends on symbol names, assembly labels, C prototypes, and data-table references must be renamed together; partial symbol renames will break linkage or table references.
Prefer character/module-specific headers over src/functions.h for prototypes once a function's home is known.
Use private double-underscore-style implementation headers, such as __ftCaptain.h, for cross-file declarations that are not meant to be broad public API.
For file-local helper functions discovered during decompilation, add static forward declarations near the top of the C file rather than exporting them globally.
A NON_MATCHING block can hold a readable C reconstruction beside an asm fallback, preserving progress while keeping the build/match safe.
For HUD digit state or similar repeated-layout data, arrays plus an enum can represent offsets more compactly than four separately named fields and can make loops easier to reconstruct.
Fixing malformed asm comments can matter for tooling that parses disassembly or m2c inputs, even if the instruction bytes are unchanged.
If a function is no longer expected to be declared as inline assembly, change its header declaration from asm void to a normal C prototype to match decompilation intent.
naming_conventions: Use ftCaptain_* rather than ftCFalcon_* for Captain Falcon functions in this codebase.
Use IfDamage* names for HUD damage-percent status structures instead of generic Hud* names when the type is specifically about percent/damage display.
Use descriptive bitfield names when known or inferred, such as randomize_velocity instead of unk40.
Use enum values Hundreds, Tens, Ones, Percent, and HUD_PLACE_MAX to index HUD damage digit arrays.
Private/internal character headers may use the pattern __ftCharacter.h, as introduced with __ftCaptain.h.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Renamed assembly globals in ftfalcon_3.s and ftfalcon_4.s to match the C-side ftCaptain_* symbol names.
Updated ftdata.s .4byte entries so callback tables reference the renamed Captain symbols.
Kept func_802F491C as asm under the default branch while placing an attempted C version under #if NON_MATCHING.
The func_802F491C C attempt models JObj assertions using __assert("jobj.h", line, "jobj") and preserves line-number comments, suggesting matching depends on inlined JObj helper behavior.
The ifstatus reconstruction uses inline helper functions for JObj translation and dirty-matrix handling to mirror expected generated code shape.
The corrupted ft_unknown_006.c asm line comment was normalized back to a valid address/comment format, likely to keep assembly/decomp tooling robust.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish or modify the NON_MATCHING func_802F491C reconstruction in ifstatus.c.
Search for remaining ftCFalcon_* symbols after PR 588 to verify Captain naming consistency.
Find subsequent changes to __ftCaptain.h to see whether this private-header pattern became standard.
Search for later cleanup of IfDamageState, IfDamageFlags, or HUD_PLACE_MAX in ifstatus.
Find PRs moving more declarations out of src/functions.h into module-specific headers.
Search for later decompilation of func_80084FA8 after its prototype changed from asm void to void.
